source: JHipster JDL reference (curated for RAG; validate in https://start.jhipster.tech/jdl-studio/ and https://www.jhipster.tech/jdl/)

chunks.title: JDL scope and tooling
chunks.body: JHipster Domain Language (JDL) describes applications, entities, fields, relationships, and options. The same grammar is edited in JDL Studio (https://start.jhipster.tech/jdl-studio/) and documented at https://www.jhipster.tech/jdl/ . A .jdl file lists declarations; order matters for relationships referencing existing entities.

chunks.title: Entity and fields
Relationships
Enums
chunks.body:
enum Language { FRENCH, ENGLISH, SPANISH }
Entity fields may use the enum type: language Language

chunks.title: Pagination, service, DTO
chunks.body:
Use: paginate * with pagination except Entity
Or: paginate EntityA, EntityB with pagination
Or: paginate Entity with infinite-scroll
Service: service all with serviceImpl except Entity, Employee
Dto: dto * with mapstruct except Entity
readOnly, skipClient, filter (JHipster options) appear as annotations on entity or field, e.g. @readOnly on entity.

chunks.title: Invalid patterns to avoid
chunks.body:
INVALID (do not generate): standalone blocks after entities such as:
Device
  lastSeen: index
  deviceId: index
That is not JDL — colons cause parse errors like "unexpected character: ->:<-".
JHipster JDL has no "field: index" lines. For MongoDB, declare fields only inside "entity Name { ... }"; add DB indexes later with @Indexed in code or Mongock, or rely on unique/required on fields.
VALID paginate example: paginate Device, SensorReading with pagination

chunks.title: Field validations
chunks.body:
Validations on fields: required, min(n), max(n), minlength(n), maxlength(n), pattern(/regex/)
Example: code String minlength(3) maxlength(20) required

chunks.title: Application configuration block
Microservices
chunks.body:
microservice Customer with gateway
searchEngine Customer with elasticsearch (when applicable)
Use applicationType microservice or gateway in application config when modeling microservice architectures.
